question_text: Which statement best describes the arrangement of particles in a solid?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles are spread out and move freely

options.label: B
options.text: Particles are closely packed in a fixed arrangement and vibrate in place

options.label: C
options.text: Particles are far apart with large spaces between them

options.label: D
options.text: Particles move randomly but stay close together

correct_answer: Particles are closely packed in a fixed arrangement and vibrate in place
explanation: In solids, particles are tightly packed together in a fixed, ordered arrangement. The particles vibrate in place but cannot move freely, which is why solids have a definite shape and volume. This is different from liquids and gases where particles have more freedom to move.
